sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 45-year-old male, presents with right elbow pain that has been present for the last three weeks.
He attributes the pain to increased tennis activity, playing with a friend at a country club.
The pain is described as sharp, stabbing, and aching, with a severity of 8/10 with use and 3/10 at rest.
The patient has tried taking Tylenol and ibuprofen, but they do not provide significant relief.
He also reports that ice does not help with the pain.
The patient has a history of diabetes type 2 and mild intermittent asthma.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of diabetes type 2, mild intermittent asthma, and an allergy to penicillin.
He had his gallbladder removed approximately 10 years ago.
Review of systems: The patient denies any fever, trauma, or recent falls.
He reports no pain radiating to the arm or shoulder.
Current medications: Metformin 500 milligrams twice a day for diabetes, Flovent twice a day for asthma, and Albuterol as needed for asthma.
Objective:
Vital signs: Not mentioned.
Physical exam: The patient has a grade 3/6 systolic ejection murmur, which is unchanged from prior exams.
He has moderate lateral epicondylar tenderness of the right elbow, with pain with passive range of motion.
There is no palpable joint effusion, but there is mild pain with resisted extension of the right wrist.
Test Results: The right elbow x-ray shows no acute fracture or other bony abnormality, no malalignment, or sign of joint effusion.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has acute lateral epicondylitis of the right elbow, also known as tennis elbow, likely due to overuse from increased tennis activity.
Problem list: Tennis elbow; diabetes type 2; mild intermittent asthma; allergy to penicillin; history of gallbladder removal.
Plan:
I prescribed Ultram 50 milligrams once a day for pain relief.
I recommend that the patient rest their right arm as much as possible, avoid repetitive stress in the right arm, and ice the right elbow for 20 minutes at least 3 times a day.
I also prescribed a brace for the right forearm to help with discomfort.
I increased the patient's metformin to 1000 milligrams twice a day for diabetes management.
I ordered a hemoglobin A1c level, CBC blood test, and chem-12 blood test.
I also prescribed a refill of Albuterol for asthma management.
Follow-up: The patient will be seen in 4 weeks to reevaluate the elbow and consider additional imaging or physical therapy if needed.
